operating_ranges.evidence: Oil-injected screw compressor specification: Oil content in air less than 3 mg/m³ at 20°C
Oil content in air less than 3 mg/m³ at 20°C

degradation_mechanisms.evidence: Oil separator element (OS) clogged. → f Have element replaced.
Every time the separator element is renewed, examine the discharge pipe and the inside of the oil separator vessel for carbon deposits; if excessive, the deposits should be removed.

fault_conditions.name: oil_separator_clogged
fault_conditions.symptoms: Compressor air output or pressure below normal
fault_conditions.evidence: Oil separator element (OS) clogged. → f Have element replaced.

maintenance_actions.name: oil_separator_element_replacement
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: oil separation efficiency and air flow capacity
maintenance_actions.evidence: Oil separator element (OS) clogged. → f Have element replaced.

maintenance_actions.name: carbon_deposit_inspection
maintenance_actions.interval: every time the separator element is renewed
maintenance_actions.what_it_resets: carbon buildup in discharge pipe and separator vessel
maintenance_actions.evidence: Every time the separator element is renewed, examine the discharge pipe and the inside of the oil separator vessel for carbon deposits; if excessive, the deposits should be removed.

assumptions.statement: Oil separator element performance degrades gradually through clogging
assumptions.reason: Manual indicates clogged element causes reduced air output/pressure and requires replacement

assumptions.statement: Carbon deposits accumulate over time in separator vessel
assumptions.reason: Manual requires inspection for carbon deposits during each element renewal
